model.label: Qwen 3 Max
model.description: Alibaba • Suitable for complex tasks, with the strongest capabilities.

pick.rationale: Purdue is a heavy favorite based on elite offensive efficiency (ranked 1st–2nd nationally) and strong defense (top 36), reflected in their consensus No. 8 overall rating. Queens, making its NCAA debut after transitioning from Division II, ranks outside the top 180 with a negative efficiency margin and a porous defense (316th per Torvik). Despite Queens winning the ASUN tournament, their profile lacks the strength of schedule or statistical foundation to challenge a Big Ten powerhouse like Purdue, which has no reported injuries or availability concerns.

reasoningStep.title: Purdue vs. Queens: Statistical Dominance and Experience Gap
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue’s elite national metrics and Big Ten pedigree vastly exceed Queens’ mid-major profile in their NCAA debut.
evidence: Purdue ranks 8th nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with the #1 offense and top-36 defense.
Queens ranks 181st–199th with a negative efficiency margin and 316th-ranked defense.
Queens is in its first NCAA tournament after moving up from D-II, while Purdue is a seasoned Big Ten champion with no reported injuries.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.title: Queens Earns #15 Seed in NCAA DI Tournament Debut; Royals Will Face #2 ...
result.snippet: NCAA Tournament Central CHARLOTTE, N.C . - The Atlantic Sun Conference Tournament champion Queens University of Charlotte men's basketball team earned the No. 15 seed in the West Region of the NCAA Tournament and will face No. 2 seed and Big Ten Tournament champion Purdue on Friday, March 20.

result.title: March Madness Contender: Queens (2026 Overview, 10-Year Snapshot ...
result.snippet: Most North Carolina-based schools have remained at the same level they chose 53 years ago. A handful, though, have opted to make bold moves up the NCAA ladder. Queens , which had made seven straight appearances in the Division Two tournament and two trips (2003, 2018) to its Final Four, became the latest school to make the jump, in 2022.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-west-8
Current game label: West 2/15
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: Purdue (purdue) vs Queens (queens)
Prior committed picks: 19
